description: Compare the values of two numbers from 1 to 20 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers from 1 to 20 written as numerals to see which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group (e.g., by using matching and counting strategies).
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group of objects has more, less, or the same number as another group by matching or counting.

subject: Math
description: Identify sets of 1 to 10 objects in patterned arrangements and tell how many without counting.
description_plain: Your child can recognize groups of 1 to 10 objects arranged in patterns and say how many there are without counting.

subject: Math
description: Say the number names in standard order when counting objects, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object. Understand that the last number name said describes the number of objects counted and that the number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted. Count out the number of objects, given a number from 1 to 20.
description_plain: Your child can count objects one by one, saying number names in order, and understands that the last number said tells how many objects there are, no matter how they are arranged.

subject: Math
description: Write whole numbers from 0 to 20 and identify number words from 0 to 10. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20, recognize number words from 0 to 10, and show how many objects match a written number.

subject: Math
description: Count to at least 100 by ones and tens. Count by one from any given number.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and tens and start counting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Students explore the foundations of numbers through counting strategies, one-to-one correspondence, and place value of numbers up to 20.
description_plain: Your child learns about numbers up to 20 by counting, matching one number to one object, and understanding place value.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices and talks about patterns they see when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for and uses patterns or structures to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the right tools to help with math tasks.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their thinking about math problems and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and ideas in different ways to understand math problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will try to understand math problems and keep working on them until they find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and graph data from observations, surveys, and investigations using scaled bar graphs and pictographs (limit scale to 2s, 5s, 10s, and 100s); interpret mathematical relationships within the data using grade-level addition, subtraction, and comparison strategies.
description_plain: Your child can collect information, organize it into bar graphs and pictures, and use addition, subtraction, and comparing to understand the data.

subject: Math
description: Students interact with a variety of data collection models and evaluate mathematical relationships within the data using grade-level appropriate strategies.
description_plain: Your child will work with different ways to collect data and use math strategies to understand the information.

subject: Math
description: Find the value of a collection of pennies, nickels, dimes, quarters, and dollars.
description_plain: Your child can count the total value of pennies, nickels, dimes, quarters, and dollar bills.

subject: Math
description: Describe relationships of time, including seconds in a minute; minutes in an hour; hours in a day; days in a week; and days, weeks, and months in a year.
description_plain: Your child understands how seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, and months relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest five minutes from analog clocks, using a.m. and p.m. Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals on the hour or half hour.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes on clocks and solve problems adding or subtracting time on the hour or half hour.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure volume (capacity) using cups and pints. Add and subtract to solve real-world problems involving capacities that are given in the same units or obtained through investigations.
description_plain: Your child can estimate and measure how much liquid something holds using cups and pints, and solve problems by adding or subtracting these amounts.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools, such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes to the nearest inch, foot, yard, centimeter, and meter.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long things are using rulers, yardsticks, or measuring tapes in inches, feet, yards, centimeters, and meters.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relationships among an inch, foot, and yard. Describe the relationship between a centimeter and meter.
description_plain: Your child understands how inches, feet, and yards relate to each other, and how centimeters and meters relate.

subject: Math
description: Students use appropriate tools, computation strategies, and relationships of measurement to solve real-world problems including measurements of length and capacity, telling time to the nearest five minutes, and collections of coins and dollars.
description_plain: Your child uses tools and math skills to solve real problems involving length, capacity, time, and money.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal parts; describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc.; and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, or four fourths. Recognize that equal parts of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into equal parts like halves and thirds, name those parts, and understand that equal parts can look different.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size (unit) squares and count to find the total number of same-size squares.
description_plain: Your child can split a rectangle into rows and columns of equal squares and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Investigate and predict the result of composing and decomposing two- and three-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child explores how two- and three-dimensional shapes can be put together or taken apart.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, and classify two- and three-dimensional shapes (i.e., triangle, square, rectangle, cube, right rectangular prism) according to the number and shape of faces and the number of sides and/or vertices. Draw two-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and describe flat and solid shapes by their sides, faces, and corners, and draw flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Students investigate and classify two- and three-dimensional shapes based on faces, sides, and vertices, and investigate the results of composing and decomposing each shape. Students continue to build foundational fraction knowledge through specific partitioning and naming of rectangles and circles.
description_plain: Your child learns about shapes by looking at their parts and how they fit together, and practices dividing shapes to understand fractions.

subject: Math
description: Create, extend, and give an appropriate rule for number patterns using addition and subtraction within 1,000.
description_plain: Your child can create and continue number patterns using addition and subtraction within 1,000 and explain the rule.

subject: Math
description: Show that the order in which two numbers are added (commutative property) and how the numbers are grouped in addition (associative property) will not change the sum. These properties can be used to show that numbers can be added in any order.
description_plain: Your child understands that changing the order or grouping of numbers when adding does not change the total.

subject: Math
description: Using number sense and place value strategies, add and subtract within 1,000, including composing and decomposing tens and hundreds. Use models, drawings, and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; describe the strategy and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1,000 using place value and different strategies, and explain how they solved the problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction within 100 in situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all parts of the addition or subtraction problem (e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem). Use estimation to decide whether answers are reasonable in addition problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems adding and subtracting within 100, using drawings or equations with unknown numbers, and check if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Within the numbers 1-100, students apply place value concepts and addition and subtraction concepts to solve real-world problems and reason about their strategies and solutions. Students explore effects of properties of addition on solutions and investigate number patterns, and apply concepts of addition and subtraction within 1,000.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and addition and subtraction skills to solve problems with numbers up to 1,000 and explains their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, using > , = , and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers using >, =, and < symbols by looking at hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Define and model a "hundred" as a group of ten tens. Model place value concepts of three-digit numbers, multiples of 100, and equivalent forms of whole numbers using objects and drawings.
description_plain: Your child understands that a hundred is ten tens and can show three-digit numbers and multiples of 100 using objects and drawings.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members (e.g., by placing that number of objects in two groups of the same size and recognizing that for even numbers no object will be left over and for odd numbers one object will be left over, or by pairing objects or counting them by twos).
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number by pairing or grouping them.

subject: Math
description: Read and write whole numbers up to 1,000. Use words, models, standard form, and expanded form to represent and show equivalent forms of whole numbers up to 1,000.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers up to 1,000 in words, numbers, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Count by ones, twos, fives, tens, and hundreds up to at least 1,000 from any given number.
description_plain: Your child can count by ones, twos, fives, tens, and hundreds up to 1,000 starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Students fluently count, read, and represent numbers up to 1,000 using place value concepts.
description_plain: Your child can count, read, and show numbers up to 1,000 using place value ideas.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices and talks about patterns they see when solving math problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for and uses patterns or structures to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the best tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child thinks about numbers and math ideas in different ways to understand problems.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child tries to understand math problems and keeps working on them until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and graph data from observations, surveys, and experiments using scaled bar graphs and pictographs. Solve real-world problems by analyzing and interpreting the data using grade-level computation and comparison strategies.
description_plain: Your child can collect, organize, and graph data using bar graphs and pictures, then solve problems by studying the data.

subject: Math
description: Students collect and present data in multiple manners and solve multi-step problems with the data.
description_plain: Your child collects data in different ways and solves multi-step problems using that data.

subject: Math
description: Find perimeters of polygons given the side lengths or given an unknown side length.
description_plain: Your child can find the perimeter of shapes by adding side lengths or figuring out missing sides.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by modeling with unit squares, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Identify and draw rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles by counting squares or multiplying side lengths and can find rectangles with the same perimeter but different areas, or the same area but different perimeters.

subject: Math
description: Find the value of any collection of coins and bills. Write amounts less than a dollar using the ¢ symbol and write larger amounts using the $ symbol in the form of dollars and cents (e.g., $4.59). Solve real-world problems to determine whether there is enough money to make a purchase.
description_plain: Your child can count the value of any coins and bills, write amounts using $ and ¢ symbols, and solve problems about having enough money to buy things.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes (e.g., by representing the problem on a number line diagram).
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute, measure time intervals, and solve problems adding or subtracting minutes.

subject: Math
description: Choose and use appropriate units and tools to estimate and measure length, weight, and temperature. Estimate and measure length to a quarter-inch, weight in pounds, and temperature in degrees Celsius and Fahrenheit.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right tools and units to estimate and measure length, weight, and temperature accurately.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure the mass of objects in grams (g) and kilograms (kg) and the volume of objects in quarts (qt), gallons (gal), and liters (l). Add, subtract, multiply, or divide to solve one-step, real-world problems involving masses or volumes that are given in the same units or obtained through investigation.
description_plain: Your child can estimate and measure how heavy objects are using grams and kilograms, and how much space they take up using quarts, gallons, and liters, then solve simple real-life problems by adding, subtracting, multiplying, or dividing these measurements.

subject: Math
description: Students use appropriate tools, computation strategies, and relationships of measurement to solve real-world problems including more specific measurements of length, weight, temperature, mass, time, and money. Students investigate and model the area of rectangles and perimeter of all polygons.
description_plain: Your child uses tools and math strategies to solve everyday problems involving length, weight, temperature, mass, time, and money, and explores how to find the area of rectangles and the perimeter of different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole (i.e., ½, 1/3, 1/4, 1/6, 1/8).
description_plain: Your child can divide shapes into equal parts and explain the size of each part using fractions like 1/2, 1/3, or 1/4.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, and draw points, lines, and line segments using appropriate tools (e.g., ruler, straightedge, and technology), and use these terms when describing two-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can find, describe, and draw points, lines, and line segments using tools like rulers, and use these words to talk about flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Define, identify, and classify four-sided shapes such as rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as quadrilaterals. Identify and draw examples and non-examples of quadrilaterals.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and sort four-sided shapes like squares, rectangles, and rhombuses, and draw examples and shapes that are not four-sided.

subject: Math
description: Students continue to investigate and classify more complex two-dimensional shapes based on their attributes.
description_plain: Your child keeps learning about different flat shapes and groups them based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Create, extend, and give an appropriate rule for number patterns within 100 (including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table).
description_plain: Your child can create and continue number patterns up to 100 and explain the rule that makes the pattern work.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving whole number multiplication and division within 100 in situations involving equal groups, arrays, and measurement quantities (e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child solves real-life problems using multiplication and division with numbers up to 100, like working with equal groups or arrays, and can use drawings or equations to show their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency with mastery of multiplication facts and corresponding division facts of 0 to 10.
description_plain: Your child knows multiplication and division facts for numbers 0 through 10 quickly and accurately.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100 using strategies such as the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., knowing that 8 x 5 = 40, one knows 40 ÷ 5 = 8) or properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100 using helpful strategies like knowing how multiplication and division are connected.

subject: Math
description: Model the concept of division of whole numbers with the following models: partitioning, sharing, and an inverse of multiplication. Model the properties of 0 and 1 in division using objects or drawings.
description_plain: Your child can show how division works by sharing or splitting groups and understands how dividing by 0 or 1 affects numbers using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Model the concept of multiplication of whole numbers using equal-sized groups, arrays, area models, and equal intervals on a number line. Model the properties of 0 and 1 in multiplication using objects or drawings.
description_plain: Your child can show how multiplication works by making equal groups, arrays, or using number lines, and understands how multiplying by 0 or 1 changes numbers using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of multi-digit whole numbers (e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child solves real-life problems by adding and subtracting multi-digit numbers, using drawings or equations with unknown numbers to explain their work.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations, and relationships between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract multi-digit numbers easily using place value and other helpful math strategies.

subject: Math
description: Students use modeling and conceptual strategies to multiply and divide numbers within 100 in real-world situations. Students apply concepts and strategies of addition and subtraction to solve real-world problems and investigate number patterns through the application of concepts of multiplication and more complex concepts of addition within 100.
description_plain: Your child uses models and thinking strategies to multiply and divide numbers up to 100 in real-life situations and applies addition and subtraction skills to solve problems and explore number patterns.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round two- and three-digit whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can round two- and three-digit numbers to the nearest ten or hundred using place value understanding.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size based on the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols > , = , or < , and justify the conclusions (e.g., by using a visual fraction model).
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions that have the same top number or bottom number by thinking about their size and use >, =, or < symbols to show which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Use fraction models to represent two simple equivalent fractions with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the quantities are the same. Use this principle to generate simple equivalent fractions (e.g., ½ = 2/4, 4/6 = 2/3).
description_plain: Your child uses fraction models to show two fractions that are equal even though they look different, and can make simple equivalent fractions like 1/2 = 2/4.

subject: Math
description: Model a non-unit fraction on a number line by marking equal lengths from 0, identifying each part as a unit fraction and locating the non-unit fraction as the endpoint on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show fractions on a number line by dividing it into equal parts and finding where the fraction ends.

subject: Math
description: Model unit fractions as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into equal parts; model non-unit fractions as the quantity formed by iterations of unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands that a unit fraction is one equal part of a whole, and other fractions are made by combining these parts.

subject: Math
description: Read and write whole numbers up to 10,000. Use words, models, standard form, and expanded form to represent and show equivalent forms of whole numbers up to 10,000.
description_plain: Your child can read and write whole numbers up to 10,000 in different ways, like words, numbers, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Students represent and round whole numbers up to 10,000. Students model, compare, and generate simple equivalent unit and non-unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child works with whole numbers up to 10,000 by representing and rounding them, and learns to model, compare, and create simple fractions that are equal.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices and explains patterns that happen again and again when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for and uses the way things are organized or connected to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking clearly and listens to others to understand their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child thinks about math problems in different ways, both with numbers and ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child works to understand math problems and keeps trying until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using data displayed in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions like 1/2, 1/4, and 1/8, and solve problems by adding or subtracting these fractions from the plot.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions that can be addressed with data. Collect, organize, and graph data from observations, surveys, and experiments using line plots with whole number intervals, single- and scaled bar graphs, and frequency tables. Solve real-world problems by analyzing and interpreting the data using grade-level computation and comparison strategies.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that can be answered with data, collects and organizes data using graphs and tables, and solves real-life problems by looking at the data.

subject: Math
description: Students collect and ask questions of the data.
description_plain: Your child learns to collect data and ask questions about it.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles to solve real-world and other mathematical problems. Investigate the area of complex shapes composed of rectangles by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles and adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts; apply this technique to solve real-world problems and other mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child uses formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles and solves problems by breaking complex shapes into rectangles and adding their areas.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve real-world problems involving distances, intervals of time, volumes, masses of objects, and money. Include addition and subtraction problems involving simple fractions and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child solves real-life problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with distances, time, volume, weight, money, and simple fractions.

subject: Math
description: Within given measurement systems, convert larger units to smaller units, including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz; l, ml; hr, min, sec., and use these conversions to solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child converts larger units to smaller units within measurement systems, like kilometers to meters or hours to minutes, and uses these conversions to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Measure length to the nearest quarter-inch, eighth-inch, and millimeter.
description_plain: Your child measures length accurately to the nearest quarter inch, eighth inch, and millimeter.

subject: Math
description: Students solve real-world problems involving distance, intervals of time, volumes, masses of objects, and money by applying computation strategies, precise measurement skills, and relationships between systems of measurement. Students continue to apply the concept of area and perimeter to complex shapes to identify solutions.
description_plain: Your child solves real-life problems involving distance, time, volume, weight, and money by measuring carefully and using math strategies, and continues working with area and perimeter of complex shapes.

subject: Math
description: Classify triangles and quadrilaterals based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or right, acute, or obtuse angles.
description_plain: Your child can sort triangles and four-sided shapes by looking for parallel or perpendicular lines and different types of angles like right, acute, or obtuse.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, and draw rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines using appropriate tools (e.g., ruler, straightedge, and technology). Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can find, describe, and draw rays, different angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines using tools, and spot these in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, and draw parallelograms, rhombuses, and trapezoids using appropriate tools (e.g., ruler, straightedge, and technology).
description_plain: Your child can find, describe, and draw shapes like parallelograms, rhombuses, and trapezoids using tools.

subject: Math
description: Students utilize appropriate tools to identify, describe, and draw parallelograms, rhombuses, and trapezoids in addition to classifying two-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child uses tools to identify, describe, and draw special four-sided shapes and sorts flat shapes based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relationship between two terms and use it to find a second number when a first number is given. Generate a number pattern that follows a given rule.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two numbers are related and use that to find a second number when given the first, and create number patterns that follow a rule.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having common denominators (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by adding and subtracting fractions that have the same bottom number, using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with common denominators (e.g., by replacing each mixed number with an equivalent fraction and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction).
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers with the same bottom number by changing them into fractions or using addition and subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with common denominators using visual fraction models. Decompose non-unit fractions to represent them as iterations of unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with the same bottom number using pictures and break down fractions into smaller parts.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems with whole numbers involving multiplicative comparison (e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem), distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems comparing amounts by multiplication, using drawings and equations with unknown numbers, and understand how this is different from adding.

subject: Math
description: Investigate the mathematical relationship between factors and multiples for whole numbers from 1-100, including the set of factors and multiples for given numbers. Identify sets of factors and multiples for any given whole number up to 100.
description_plain: Your child can find and list all the factors and multiples of whole numbers up to 100 and understand how factors and multiples are related.

subject: Math
description: Show how the order in which two numbers are multiplied (commutative property) and how numbers are grouped in multiplication (associative property) will not change the product. Use these properties to show that numbers can be multiplied in any order. Investigate and apply the distributive property.
description_plain: Your child understands that changing the order or grouping of numbers when multiplying does not change the answer and can use this to multiply numbers in any order.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Describe the strategy and explain the reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers with up to four digits by one-digit numbers, find the answer and remainder, and explain how they solved it.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Describe the strategy and explain the reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers using place value and explain their method.

subject: Math
description: Students solve real-world problems using place value strategies and properties of multiplication and division with limitations. Students compose (addition) and decompose (subtraction) non-unit fractions and mixed numbers using models and strategies, applying these concepts to real-world situations. Students investigate the relationship between two given sets of numbers and generate number patterns based upon given rules.
description_plain: Your child solves real-life problems using place value and multiplication/division rules, works with fractions and mixed numbers using models, and finds number patterns based on rules.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any given place value.
description_plain: Your child can round large whole numbers to any place value using their understanding of place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size based on the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols > , = , or < , and justify the conclusions (e.g., by using a visual model).
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to hundredths by thinking about their size, write >, =, or < to show the comparison, and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Write tenths and hundredths in decimal and fraction notations. Use words, models, standard form, and expanded form to represent decimal numbers to hundredths. Mentally calculate fraction and decimal equivalents for halves and fourths (e.g., ½ = 0.5 = 0.50, 7/4 = 1¾ = 1.75).
description_plain: Your child can write tenths and hundredths as decimals and fractions, use words and models to show these numbers, and quickly find decimal and fraction equivalents for halves and fourths.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators (e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark, such as 0, ½, and 1). Explain why comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols > , = , or < , and justify the conclusions (e.g., by using a visual fraction model).
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different top and bottom numbers by making them easier to compare and explain why comparisons only work when the fractions are parts of the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Use fraction models to represent two equivalent fractions with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to generate equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use fraction models to show two fractions that look different but are equal and use this idea to create equivalent fractions.

subject: Math
description: Model mixed numbers and improper fractions using visual fraction models such as number lines and area models. Use a visual fraction model to show the equivalency between whole numbers and whole numbers as fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures like number lines and shapes to show mixed numbers and improper fractions and explain how whole numbers can be shown as fractions.

subject: Math
description: Read and write whole numbers up to 1,000,000. Use words, models, standard form, and expanded form to represent and show equivalent forms of whole numbers up to 1,000,000.
description_plain: Your child can read and write whole numbers up to 1,000,000 using words, pictures, and different number forms.

subject: Math
description: Students represent and round multi-digit numbers. Students model, compare, and generate equivalent fractions, mixed numbers, and decimal numbers to the tenths and hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can work with large numbers, round them, and model, compare, and create equal fractions, mixed numbers, and decimals to tenths and hundredths.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns that happen again and again when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can look for and use the way things are organized or connected to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their thinking clearly and listen to others to understand and improve ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and problems in different ways to understand and solve them.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep trying different ways until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Calculate measures of central tendency (mean, median, and mode) to describe a data set. Analyze data sets to determine which measure of central tendency appropriately describes the distribution of data.
description_plain: Your child can find the average, middle, and most common numbers in a data set and decide which one best describes the data.

subject: Math
description: Formulate questions that can be addressed with categorical and numerical data and make predictions about the data. Collect, organize, and graph data from observations, surveys, and experiments using line plots with fractional intervals, histograms, or other graphical representations that appropriately represent the data set.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that can be answered with data, make guesses about the data, and collect, organize, and show data using graphs like line plots and histograms.

subject: Math
description: Students create questions appropriate to the data and answer the questions using multiple representations.
description_plain: Your child can create questions about data and answer them using different ways to show the data.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = B × h for right rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths to solve real-world problems and other mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of boxes with whole-number sides to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths or multiplying the height by the area of the base.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a box by filling it with unit cubes and show that this matches multiplying the side lengths or height times base area.

subject: Math
description: Develop and use formulas for the area of triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids. Solve real-world and other mathematical problems that involve perimeter and area of triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids, using appropriate units for measures.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use formulas to find the area of triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids and solve problems about their perimeter and area.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths by modeling with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional side lengths by using unit squares and multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change between different measurement units in the same system and use these changes to solve multi-step real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Students investigate the volume of rectangular prisms and solve real-world problems through the development and application of area formulas for rectangles, triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids. Students investigate and convert measurements within the Customary and metric measurement systems.
description_plain: Your child explores volume of boxes, solves problems using area formulas for shapes, and converts measurements in both Customary and metric systems.

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, and draw triangles (right, acute, obtuse) and circles using appropriate tools (e.g., ruler or straightedge, compass, and technology). Define and model the relationship between radius and diameter.
description_plain: Your child can identify, describe, and draw different types of triangles and circles using tools like rulers and compasses, and understand how radius and diameter relate.

subject: Math
description: Students use appropriate tools to investigate attributes of triangles and circles.
description_plain: Your child uses tools to explore the features of triangles and circles.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world problems and equations by graphing ordered pairs in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane, and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can graph points in the first part of the coordinate plane to show real-life problems and explain what the points mean.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with decimals to hundredths including problems that involve money in decimal notation (e.g., by using equations, models or drawings, and strategies based on place value or properties of operations to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with decimals to hundredths, including money, using equations, drawings, and place value strategies.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using models or drawings and strategies based on place value or the properties of operations. Describe the strategy and explain the reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using models and place value strategies and explain how they solved the problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving division of fractions and mixed numbers (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems that involve dividing fractions and mixed numbers using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions, including mixed numbers (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems that involve multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Use visual fraction models and numbers to divide a fraction by a fraction or a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and numbers to divide one fraction by another fraction or by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Use visual fraction models to multiply a fraction by a fraction or a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures to multiply a fraction by another fraction or by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators (e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem). Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess whether the answer is reasonable.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators using pictures and equations, and can estimate if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with unlike denominators using strategies or the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers even when the denominators are different, using different methods.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication and division of whole numbers (e.g., by using equations to represent the problem). In division problems that involve a remainder, explain how the remainder affects the solution to the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems involving multiplying and dividing whole numbers, and explain what a remainder means in division.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Describe the strategy and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by two-digit numbers using place value and multiplication ideas, and explain how they solved it.

subject: Math
description: Students apply concepts and strategies of multiplication and division to solve real-world problems. Students add and subtract unlike fractions and use visual fraction models to multiply and divide fractions and whole numbers. Students apply conceptual models and strategies to all operations with decimals to solve real-world problems and represent real-world situations within the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems using multiplication and division, add and subtract fractions with different denominators, and use pictures to multiply and divide fractions and whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Model percents as parts of 100 using pictures or diagrams and identify the equivalent fraction.
description_plain: Your child can show percents as parts of 100 using pictures and find the matching fraction.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain how multiplying by 10, 100, or 1000 changes the number of zeros or moves the decimal point, and use exponents to show powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Explain different interpretations of fractions, including as parts of a whole, parts of a set, and division of whole numbers by whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands that fractions can mean parts of a whole, parts of a group, or the result of dividing whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use a number line to compare and order fractions, mixed numbers, and decimals to thousandths. Write the results using > , = , and < symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use a number line to compare and order fractions, mixed numbers, and decimals, and use >, =, and < symbols to show the comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Students explore place value through representing powers of 10 as exponents, modeling percents as parts of 100, and comparing and ordering fractions, mixed numbers, and decimals to the thousandth.
description_plain: Your child learns about place value with powers of 10, shows percents as parts of 100, and compares fractions, mixed numbers, and decimals up to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns when solving problems repeatedly.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use the structure or pattern in math problems to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like rulers or calculators, to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking clearly and listen to others’ ideas to decide if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, both with numbers and ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep trying different ways to solve them.

subject: Math
description: For events with a large number of outcomes, understand the use of the multiplication counting principle. Develop the multiplication counting principle, and apply it to situations with a large number of outcomes.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication to count all possible outcomes when there are many choices.

subject: Math
description: Define the probability of a compound event, just as with simple events, as the fraction of outcomes in the sample space for which the compound event occurs. Use appropriate terminology to describe independent, dependent, complementary, and mutually exclusive events.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of combined events happening and use words like independent, dependent, complementary, and mutually exclusive to describe them.

subject: Math
description: Represent sample spaces and find probabilities of compound events (independent and dependent) using organized lists, tables, and tree diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can list all possible outcomes and find chances of combined events using lists, tables, or tree diagrams.

subject: Math
description: Write and use equations that model linear relationships to make predictions, including interpolation and extrapolation, in real-world situations involving bivariate measurement data. Interpret the slope and y-intercept in context.
description_plain: Your child can write and use equations to show relationships between two things and make predictions from graphs or tables.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantitative variables. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child can make and read scatter plots to find patterns between two sets of data, like spotting groups or trends.

subject: Math
description: Students begin to investigate and represent bivariate data using scatter plots.They build on their experience with univariate data. Students also build on the probability work in grade seven to examine and represent the probability and compound events.
description_plain: Your child learns to use scatter plots to study two sets of data and builds on previous work with probability and combined events.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in real-world and other mathematical problems in two dimensions.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles for real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and other mathematical problems involving volume of cones, spheres, and pyramids and surface area of spheres.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the volume of cones, spheres, and pyramids, and the surface area of spheres.

subject: Math
description: Explore dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections on two-dimensional figures in the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can explore moving shapes by sliding, turning, flipping, and resizing them on a grid.

subject: Math
description: Students explore transformations in the coordinate plane and are also expected to understand and explain the Pythagorean Theorem, its converse, and to use this relationship to solve problems and find distance on the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child learns about moving shapes on a grid and uses the Pythagorean Theorem to solve problems and find distances.

subject: Math
description: Approximate the solution of a system of equations by graphing and interpreting the reasonableness of the approximation.
description_plain: Your child can estimate where two lines cross by graphing and decide if the answer makes sense.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two linear functions given in different forms, such as a table of values, equation, verbal description, and graph (e.g., compare a distance-time graph to a distance-time equation to determine which of two moving objects has greater speed).
description_plain: Your child can compare two lines shown in different ways, like graphs or tables, to see which one changes faster.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities given a verbal description, table of values, or graph. Within the context of a problem, describe the meaning of m (rate of change) and b (y-intercept) in y = mx + b.
description_plain: Your child can create a rule for a straight-line relationship from words, tables, or graphs and explain what the rate of change and starting point mean.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function whose graph is a straight line; give examples of functions that are not linear. Describe similarities and differences between linear and nonlinear functions from tables, graphs, verbal descriptions, and equations.
description_plain: Your child understands that y = mx + b makes a straight line and can tell the difference between straight-line and other types of functions.

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (e.g., where the function is increasing or decreasing, linear or nonlinear, has a maximum or minimum value). Sketch a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function that has been verbally described.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a graph changes, like going up or down, and draw a graph based on a description.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function assigns to each x-value (independent variable) exactly one y-value (dependent variable), and that the graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs (x,y).
description_plain: Your child understands that a function gives one output for each input and that its graph shows all input-output pairs.

subject: Math
description: Generate linear equations in one variable with one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solutions. Justify the classification given.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve equations that have one solution, many solutions, or no solutions, and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations and inequalities with rational number coefficients fluently, including those whose solutions require expanding expressions using the distributive property and collecting like terms. Represent real-world problems using linear equations and inequalities in one variable and solve such problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations and inequalities with fractions and use them to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Students understand the formal definition of a function, analyze linear functions in multiple representations, and differentiate between linear and nonlinear functions. Students also solve a system of linear equations in two unknowns.
description_plain: Your child understands what a function is, studies straight-line functions in different ways, tells linear and nonlinear functions apart, and solves systems of two linear equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems with rational numbers by using multiple operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems using rational numbers and different math operations.

subject: Math
description: Given a numeric expression with common rational number bases and integer exponents, apply the properties of exponents to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about exponents to rewrite expressions with rational numbers and powers.

subject: Math
description: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare the size of irrational numbers, plot them approximately on a number line, and estimate the value of expressions involving irrational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use close decimal guesses to compare and place irrational numbers on a number line and estimate answers with them.

subject: Math
description: Give examples of rational and irrational numbers, and explain the difference between them. State decimal equivalents for any number. For rational numbers, show that the decimal equivalent terminates or repeats, and convert a repeating decimal into a rational number.
description_plain: Your child can give examples of rational and irrational numbers, explain how they are different, and show decimal forms for any number, including turning repeating decimals into fractions.

subject: Math
description: Students continue to deepen their understanding of rational and irrational numbers by explaining the differences between them and solving real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between rational and irrational numbers and uses this knowledge to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child notices patterns when solving problems and explains how these patterns help.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child looks for and uses the structure or organization in math problems to solve them more easily.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses and uses the right tools, like calculators or rulers, to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child explains their math thinking clearly and listens to others to understand and improve their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child thinks about math problems both in general ideas and with specific numbers to find solutions.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child understands math problems and keeps trying different ways to solve them until they find an answer.

subject: Math
description: Summarize bivariate categorical data in two-way frequency tables. Interpret relative frequencies in the contexts of the data (including joint, marginal, and conditional relative frequencies). Recognize possible associations and trends in data.
description_plain: Your child can organize data about two categories in tables, understand different types of frequencies, and spot patterns or connections in the data.

subject: Math
description: Summarize bivariate categorical data in two-way frequency tables. Interpret relative frequencies in the contexts of the data (including joint, marginal, and conditional relative frequencies). Recognize possible associations and trends in data.
description_plain: Your child can organize data about two categories in tables, understand different types of frequencies, and spot patterns or connections in the data.

subject: Math
description: Summarize bivariate categorical data in two-way frequency tables. Interpret relative frequencies in the contexts of the data (including joint, marginal, and conditional relative frequencies). Recognize possible associations and trends in data.
description_plain: Your child can organize data about two categories in tables, understand different types of frequencies, and spot patterns or connections in the data.

subject: Math
description: Summarize bivariate categorical data in two-way frequency tables. Interpret relative frequencies in the contexts of the data (including joint, marginal, and conditional relative frequencies). Recognize possible associations and trends in data.
description_plain: Your child can organize data about two categories in tables, understand different types of frequencies, and spot patterns or connections in the data.

subject: Math
description: Use technology to find a linear function that models a relationship between two quantitative variables to make predictions and interpret the slope and y-intercept. Using technology, compute and interpret the correlation coefficient.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to find and understand a straight-line equation that shows how two things relate, and explains what the slope and starting point mean.

subject: Math
description: Use technology to find a linear function that models a relationship between two quantitative variables to make predictions and interpret the slope and y-intercept. Using technology, compute and interpret the correlation coefficient.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to find and understand a straight-line equation that shows how two things relate, and explains what the slope and starting point mean.

subject: Math
description: Use technology to find a linear function that models a relationship between two quantitative variables to make predictions and interpret the slope and y-intercept. Using technology, compute and interpret the correlation coefficient.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to find and understand a straight-line equation that shows how two things relate, and explains what the slope and starting point mean.

subject: Math
description: Use technology to find a linear function that models a relationship between two quantitative variables to make predictions and interpret the slope and y-intercept. Using technology, compute and interpret the correlation coefficient.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to find and understand a straight-line equation that shows how two things relate, and explains what the slope and starting point mean.

subject: Math
description: Understand that statistics and data are non-neutral and designed to serve a particular interest. Analyze the possibilities for whose interest might be served and how the representations might be misleading.
description_plain: Your child learns that statistics can be biased and thinks about who might benefit from the way data is shown and how it might be misleading.

subject: Math
description: Understand that statistics and data are non-neutral and designed to serve a particular interest. Analyze the possibilities for whose interest might be served and how the representations might be misleading.
description_plain: Your child learns that statistics can be biased and thinks about who might benefit from the way data is shown and how it might be misleading.

subject: Math
description: Understand that statistics and data are non-neutral and designed to serve a particular interest. Analyze the possibilities for whose interest might be served and how the representations might be misleading.
description_plain: Your child learns that statistics can be biased and thinks about who might benefit from the way data is shown and how it might be misleading.

subject: Math
description: Understand that statistics and data are non-neutral and designed to serve a particular interest. Analyze the possibilities for whose interest might be served and how the representations might be misleading.
description_plain: Your child learns that statistics can be biased and thinks about who might benefit from the way data is shown and how it might be misleading.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statistics as a process for making inferences about a population based on a random sample from that population. Recognize the purposes of and differences among sample surveys, experiments, and observational studies; explain how randomization relates to each.
description_plain: Your child understands how statistics help make guesses about a whole group by looking at a random sample, and knows the difference between surveys, experiments, and observations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Define the term citizens and describe the characteristics of good citizenship. (E)
description_plain: Your child knows what a citizen is and what it means to be a good citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe ways that individual actions can contribute to the common good of the classroom or community.
description_plain: Your child can describe ways their actions can help their classroom or community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Define and give examples of rules and laws in the school and the community, and explain the benefits of these rules and laws.

subject: Social Studies

subject: Social Studies
description: Develop a simple timeline of important events in the student's life. (E)

subject: Social Studies

subject: Math
description: Students apply the logic of geometric proofs, including in core concepts related to angles and lines.
description_plain: Your child uses logical reasoning to create geometric proofs, especially about angles and lines.

subject: Math
description: Students apply the logic of geometric proofs, including in core concepts related to angles and lines.
description_plain: Your child uses logical reasoning to create geometric proofs, especially about angles and lines.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify ways that societies deal with helpful and harmful externalities in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific. (E)
description_plain: Your child can identify how societies in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific handle positive and negative effects that impact others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Illustrate how international trade requires a system for exchanging currency between and among countries.
description_plain: Your child explains how countries trade internationally by exchanging different currencies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how voluntary trade benefits countries and results in higher standards of living in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific. (E)
description_plain: Your child explains how countries benefit from trading voluntarily and how this improves living standards in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students examine the influence of physical and cultural factors upon the economic systems found in countries of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific, with an emphasis on scarcity, incentive, opportunity cost, and specialization.
description_plain: Your child studies how physical and cultural factors affect the economies of countries in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific, focusing on scarcity, choices, and specialization.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze current issues and developments related to the environment in selected countries in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.
description_plain: Your child analyzes current environmental issues in selected countries in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Provide examples of ethnocentrism and how this attitude affected the relationships between different peoples in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.
description_plain: Your child gives examples of ethnocentrism and explains how it affected relationships between different groups in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify current trends and patterns of rural and urban population distribution in selected countries of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific, and analyze the causes for these patterns.
description_plain: Your child identifies current patterns of rural and city populations in selected countries and explains why these patterns happen.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the limitations that climate and landforms place on land or people in regions of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.
description_plain: Your child describes how climate and land shapes limit land use or people in regions of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast the distribution of natural resources in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific; describe how natural resource distribution can impact the wealth of a country. (E)
description_plain: Your child compares where natural resources are found in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific and explains how this affects a country's wealth.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify major physical characteristics of regions of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific, such as deserts, basins, plains, mountains, and rivers, and describe their formation.
description_plain: Your child identifies major land features like deserts, mountains, and rivers in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific and describes how they formed.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use historical maps to identify changes in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific over time. (E)
description_plain: Your child uses old maps to see how Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific have changed over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe and compare major cultural characteristics of regions in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.
description_plain: Your child describes and compares important cultural traits of regions in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Formulate a broad understanding of the location of countries within Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific. (E)
description_plain: Your child learns where countries are located within Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students use technology and grid systems to identify and categorize places (physical, cultural, countries, large cities), major geographic characteristics (human and physical), and regions in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific. They use geographic skills, perspectives, and technologies to analyze relationships within and between these regions and the rest of the world.
description_plain: Your child uses technology and maps to find and organize places, land features, and regions in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific, and studies how these relate to each other and the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how major forms of governments of Japan, North Korea, India, South Africa, and China currently protect or violate the human rights of their citizens. (E)
description_plain: Your child describes how governments in Japan, North Korea, India, South Africa, and China protect or violate human rights today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast historical and contemporary governments in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.
description_plain: Your child compares past and present governments in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare, contrast, and evaluate the different routes to independence from colonial rule taken by countries in Asia, Africa, and the Southwest Pacific. (E)
description_plain: Your child compares and evaluates how countries in Asia, Africa, and the Southwest Pacific gained independence from colonial rule.

subject: Social Studies
description: Students trace the development of different forms of government in different historical eras and compare various contemporary political structures in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific in terms of power, approach to human rights, and the roles of citizens.
description_plain: Your child can follow how different governments have changed over time and compare how countries in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific share power, treat human rights, and involve their citizens today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast perspectives of history in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific using fictional and nonfictional accounts, including visual, literary, art, and musical sources.
description_plain: Your child will compare different stories and viewpoints about history in Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific using books, pictures, music, and art.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain recent conflicts and political issues between nations or cultural groups and evaluate the solutions that different organizations have utilized to address these conflicts. (E)
description_plain: Your child can identify recent conflicts between countries or groups and explain how different organizations have tried to solve these problems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain the significance of historical events in the Middle East since the end of World War II.
description_plain: Your child understands important events in the Middle East since World War II and why they matter.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the Japanese imperial period (1868-1945), including Japan's involvement in World War II.
description_plain: Your child can study Japan’s history from 1868 to 1945, including its role in World War II.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the reasons for European colonization of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific and analyze the long- and short-term impact that colonization and imperialism had on the social, political, and economic development of these societies from both European and indigenous perspectives. (E)
description_plain: Your child will explain why Europeans colonized parts of Africa, Asia, and the Southwest Pacific and how this affected those societies both quickly and over time, from both European and local viewpoints.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze worldwide voyages of exploration and discovery by considering multiple perspectives of various people in the past by demonstrating their differing motives, beliefs, interests, hopes, and fears.
description_plain: Your child can explore worldwide explorations by looking at different people’s reasons, beliefs, hopes, and fears from the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate how Japan became increasingly independent of earlier Chinese influences and developed its own political, religious, social, and artistic traditions.
description_plain: Your child understands how Japan became more independent from China and created its own government, religion, society, and art.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the dynastic cycle and the cultural and technological contributions of major Chinese dynasties (Zhou, Qin, Han, Tang, Song, and Ming). (E)
description_plain: Your child can describe how Chinese dynasties rose and fell and what important cultural and technological ideas they contributed.

subject: Social Studies
description: Trace the rise, spread, and influence of the Mongols.
